title: Former student eligibility policy and deactivation timeline for M365 Services
body: Students who leave or graduate from UW-Madison no longer have indefinite access to Microsoft 365 (M365) services. Those who graduate or leave UW will lose access to their email account and other Microsoft 365 data 9 months after leaving the university. The scope and timeline of these changes are detailed below. This timeline includes changes to the following IT Services: NetID@wisc.edu email account access and data M365 Applications (Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, Word, Excel, etc.) access and data Your university status has changed or will change due to the following: You have completed your degree program and graduated from the university or You are no longer enrolled as a student with the university Because your university affiliation is changing, your eligibility for IT services provided by UW-Madison will also change. You will continue to be eligible for M365 Services for a 6-month grace period after graduating or leaving the university. This grace period is designed to give you time to get your files and data moved to a new personal or professional account. After the 6-month grace period ends, you will no longer have access to UW-Madison M365 services, and your account will enter the deactivation process. Deactivation Timeline Day Event Change Day 0 Graduate or withdrawal None Day 90* License Change Microsoft 365 - Affiliation Groups and Access to Microsoft 365 Services Day 180 Notification #1 sent Begins deactivation process Day 210 Notification #2 sent None Day 240 Notification #3 sent None Day 255 Notification #4 sent None Day 270 Deactivation Account is no longer accessible to the former student If you have an ongoing collaboration with a university department or faculty member that requires you to maintain access to your M365 account, you may be eligible for an extension for access to these services. Extensions will only be permitted if requested before the account deactivation date (day 270 above). Please contact the DoIT Help Desk to request an extension if you meet these criteria. As of June 10, 2024 accounts which have already been deactivated will no longer be reactivated upon request. *License Change happens on or around Day 90 and commonly transitions users from Group A/B to Group D/E if there is no continued affiliation with the university. Please see the linked KB doc for additional information.
